description_long: This is a classic biplane with two stacked wings, painted in a combination of green, blue, and black with white and yellow accents. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers towards the tail, which features a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer with rounded tips. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail wheel, all fixed and exposed. The cockpit is positioned just behind the upper wing, appearing to be open and likely designed for a single pilot. The wings are straight with no visible sweep or dihedral, and there are no winglets present. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, suitable for display or flight in open urban parks or airfields.
description: A vintage-style biplane with green and blue coloring, dual stacked wings, and an open cockpit behind the upper wing. It features a front-mounted propeller and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, central propeller, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the upper and lower wings, open cockpit, and streamlined fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizer, and the full span of both wings.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the tail section, wing structure, and cockpit placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, likely single-seat.
